1.question: According to Butler, performativity and precarity become linked because:

options.A: Both concepts disregard the role of social norms
options.B: Gender performance determines who counts as a recognizable subject
options.C: Precarity eliminates all forms of gender identity
options.D: Performativity guarantees equal recognition to all identities

1.correctOption: Gender performance determines who counts as a recognizable subject

2.question: Which philosopher does Butler draw upon when discussing the idea of “the right to have rights”?

3.question: Butler critiques identity politics because it:

options.A: Works best through strict gender binaries
options.B: Reduces diverse lived experiences into a singular group identity
options.C: Ensures universal equality for all identities
options.D: Eliminates the role of public assembly

3.correctOption: Reduces diverse lived experiences into a singular group identity

4.question: The “right to appear,” as discussed by Butler, refers to:

options.A: The ability for individuals to be visibly present in public without fear of violence or erasure
options.B: A privilege granted solely through medical documentation
options.C: A symbolic right with no connection to lived experience
options.D: A cultural tradition unrelated to gender politics

4.correctOption: The ability for individuals to be visibly present in public without fear of violence or erasure

5.question: The suffrage movement is significant because it:

options.A: Focused only on access to education
options.B: Secured women's right to vote after decades of struggle
options.C: Originated in Europe during the 21st century
options.D: Eliminated all forms of gender discrimination globally

5.correctOption: Secured women's right to vote after decades of struggle

6.question: The MeToo movement originally began in 2006 with the work of:

7.question: Max Weber’s concept of charismatic authority emphasizes leadership based on:

8.question: The example of Rosa Parks refusing to give up her seat illustrates:

options.A: A tactic of creating a powerful and symbolic origin story
options.B: A spontaneous action with no strategic significance
options.C: A fictional narrative used only in academic theory
options.D: A failure of social movement strategy

8.correctOption: A tactic of creating a powerful and symbolic origin story

9.question: One major criticism of the mainstream #MeToo movement is that it:

options.A: Has focused primarily on privileged voices, excluding many marginalized survivors
options.B: Was entirely led by nonprofit organizations
options.D: Focused only on LGBTQIA advocacy and ignored women

9.correctOption: Has focused primarily on privileged voices, excluding many marginalized survivors

10.question: One documented backlash effect after #MeToo was that some senior male professionals:

options.A: Began avoiding one-on-one meetings with junior female colleagues due to fear or discomfort
options.B: Increased gender equity training in their companies

10.correctOption: Began avoiding one-on-one meetings with junior female colleagues due to fear or discomfort
